evals.prompt: Our test suite started failing right after a dependency bump. Three integration tests now time out. Please debug the root cause before we change any code.
evals.expected_output: This should trigger systematic-debugging because the task is explicitly about investigating a test regression and finding root cause before attempting fixes. The response should center investigation and evidence gathering rather than jumping into a speculative patch.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_true
assertions.description: This prompt should trigger systematic-debugging because it explicitly asks for root-cause investigation on failing tests

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response explains that the task is a debugging workflow over a regression rather than a feature request

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response stays focused on investigation-first debugging instead of immediately proposing code changes

evals.prompt: After yesterday's deploy, checkout sometimes double-charges and logs show retries from multiple services. Help me debug what is actually breaking before we decide on a fix.
evals.expected_output: This should trigger systematic-debugging because the task describes unexpected runtime behavior in a multi-component flow and explicitly asks to debug what is breaking before selecting a fix. The response should frame the work as root-cause investigation.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_true
assertions.description: This prompt should trigger systematic-debugging because it is a production debugging request over a multi-component failure

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response identifies unexpected production behavior and investigation-before-fix as the key routing signals

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response does not prematurely prescribe retries, locks, or architecture changes before investigation

evals.prompt: The build passes locally but fails in CI with missing env vars and a signing error. Investigate where the breakage happens.
evals.expected_output: This should trigger systematic-debugging because the task is to investigate a build failure and identify where configuration or environment propagation is breaking. The response should emphasize evidence collection across component boundaries.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_true
assertions.description: This prompt should trigger systematic-debugging because it asks to investigate a CI build failure and locate the failure point

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response explains that the request is a debugging investigation over environment propagation and build failure

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response stays within debugging workflow guidance instead of switching into implementation planning

evals.prompt: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for the payment API.
evals.expected_output: This should NOT trigger systematic-debugging because the request is phrased as direct implementation work rather than root-cause investigation over a bug or failure. The more appropriate route is test-driven-development or direct execution.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_false
assertions.description: This prompt should not trigger systematic-debugging because it is a direct implementation request, not a debugging investigation

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response explains that no bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior is being investigated here

assertions.name: routing_preferred_skill_test-driven-development
assertions.description: The response points to test-driven-development as the better fit for implementing the requested behavior change

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response does not manufacture a debugging workflow for a straightforward implementation request

evals.prompt: Review docs/design-decisions/2026-03-30-design-skill-system-redesign.md for missing rollout decisions and compatibility risks.
evals.expected_output: This should NOT trigger systematic-debugging because the task is a document audit over design decisions, not a technical issue investigation. The more appropriate route is design-decision-audit.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_false
assertions.description: This prompt should not trigger systematic-debugging because it is a design review request rather than a runtime or test failure investigation

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response explains that the task concerns missing design decisions rather than root-cause debugging

assertions.name: routing_preferred_skill_design-decision-audit
assertions.description: The response points to design-decision-audit as the better fit for reviewing rollout and compatibility risks

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response does not force a document audit into a debugging workflow

evals.prompt: Take this messy multi-part request and turn it into a task brief for another agent.
evals.expected_output: This should NOT trigger systematic-debugging because the user is asking for request normalization and handoff preparation, not investigation of a technical failure. The more appropriate route is task-brief.

assertions.name: routing_should_trigger_false
assertions.description: This prompt should not trigger systematic-debugging because there is no bug, failure, or unexpected behavior to investigate

assertions.name: routing_reason_matches_boundary
assertions.description: The response explains that the task is about structuring a request rather than debugging a technical issue

assertions.name: routing_preferred_skill_task-brief
assertions.description: The response points to task-brief as the better fit for producing a structured handoff brief

assertions.name: routing_output_stays_in_scope
assertions.description: The response does not invent a debugging workflow when the task is actually request normalization
